page_content:
GEOL443 Petrology (4 Credits)

Study of igneous and metamorphic rocks: petrogenesis, distributions, chemical and mineralogical relations, macroscopic and microscopic descriptions, geologic significance.

Prerequisite: GEOL322. And CHEM131 and CHEM132; or (CHEM135 and CHEM136); or CHEM103. And must have completed or be concurrently enrolled in GEOL423; and (GEOL100 or GEOL120); and GEOL110. Corequisite: Permission of CMNS-Geology department.

Restriction: Non-degree-seeking students require the permission of the instructor.
